stemHtml: In a mountainous area, the most hazardous flying conditions associated with standing waves are likely to be experienced:

options.a: in the vicinity of the "roll" cloud or rotor zone beneath the first wave on the leeward side.
options.b: between the troughs of the waves particularly 30 NM to 50 NM downwind and close to the tropopause.
options.c: just above the lenticular cloud on the windward side.
options.d: on the windward side of the mountain just above the "cap" cloud.
